title: [Comb] Canonicalize using De Morgan's laws
body:
I just realized on an example that we were emitting `~(~a | ~b)` in Verilog. Could it be that we are missing De Morgan's laws as canonicalizations to reduce the number of inversions in an expression? For example, the above begs to be emitted as `a & b`.

### Example
```mlir
// foo.mlir
hw.module @Foo(%a: i1, %b: i1) -> (c: i1) {
  %true = hw.constant true
  %0 = comb.xor %true, %a : i1
  %1 = comb.xor %true, %b : i1
  %2 = comb.or %0, %1 : i1
  %3 = comb.xor %true, %2 : i1
  hw.output %3 : i1
}
```
```systemverilog
// firtool --verilog foo.mlir
module Foo(
  input  a, b,
  output c);

  assign c = ~(~a | ~b);
endmodule
```
